findings.one_line: The claimed stochastic scale `n^{-1/2} q^{-1/2}` uses `Var(Gamma_q|X) \lesssim 1/max{p_P,q}`, but from the declared assumptions I can only reproduce the crude `O(q^{-2})` envelope; the stronger bound needs the omitted estimator-side assumption `ass:clipped-propensity-variance-stability` or an equivalent derivation.

Reproducing the ERM basic inequality yields an additional fluctuation term `sup_pi |(P_n-P)[(pi-pi_star)(tau_P+b_q)]|`; the proof keeps only the centered clipped-score process plus additive drift, so the stochastic decomposition is incomplete.

The proof route invokes `lem:clipped-score-drift-bound`, `lem:clipped-score-variance-envelope`, `lem:feasible-master-bound`, and `lem:clip-balance-exponent`, but these are neither declared dependencies nor core nodes, so the upper-rate balance is not discharged on the DAG.
The headline two-sided bracket inherits the failed upper route, so the asserted bound `M_n(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma,a,c)} (log n)^p` is not proved as stated.

This node defines `r_feas` via the balance `g(a,c,gamma)`, but the core's symbol layer still defines `s_feas,r_feas` through an undeclared `kappa_mu`; the feasible exponent exported downstream is therefore inconsistent across the core.
